title: sidalcea neomexicana subsp. thurberi
intro: Sidalcea neomexicana subsp. thurberi, also known as Thurber's checkerbloom, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Thurber's checkerbloom include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and are often used to treat a variety of ailments, including skin irritations and infections.
